operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\\n83:  * Trigger}.\\n84:  *\\n85:  * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\\n86:  * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\\n87:  * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\\n88:  * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\\n89:  * WindowAssigner}.\\n90:  *\\n91:  * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}.          && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\\n534:             clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\\n535:         }\\n536: \\n537: .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\\n550:                 .build();\\n551:     }\\n552: \\n553:     /**\\n554:      * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link triggerContext object.\\n559:      */\\n560:     private void clearAllState(\\n561:             W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\\n562: (mergingWindows != null) {\\n568:             mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\\n569:             mergingWindows.persist();\\n570:         }\\n571:     }\\n572: \\n573:     /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\\n574:     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n575:     private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\\n580:     }\\n581: \\n582:     /**\\n583:      * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\\n584:      *\\n585: element);\\n589:     }\\n590: \\n591:     /**\\n592:      * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. \\n645:     /**\\n646:      * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\\n647:      *\\n648:      * @param window the window whose state to discard\\n649:      */\\n650: java.util.Collection;\\n77: \\n78: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\\n79: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\\n80: \\n81: /**\\n82:  * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\\n83:  * Trigger}.\\n84:  *\\n85:  * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\\n86:  * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\\n87:  * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\\n88:  * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\\n89:  * WindowAssigner}.\\n90:  *\\n91:  * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\\n92:  * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\\n93:  * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\\n94:  * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\\n95:  *\\n96:  * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\\n97:  * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\\n98:  * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\\n99:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\\n100:  */\\n101: @Internal\\n102: public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\\n103:         extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\\n104:         implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\\n105: \\n106:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\\n107: \\n108:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n109:     // Configuration values and user functions\\n110:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n111: \\n112:     protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\\n113: \\n114:     private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\\n115: \\n116:     private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\\n117: \\n118:     private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\\n119: \\n120:     /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\\n121:     protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\\n122: \\n123:     /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\\n124:     protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\\n125: \\n126:     /**\\n127:      * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\\n128:      *\\n129:      * <ul>\\n130:      *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\\n131:      *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\\n132:      *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\\n133:      * </ul>\\n134:      */\\n135:     protected final long allowedLateness;\\n136: \\n137:     /**\\n138:      * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\\n139:      * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\\n140:      * to this.\\n141:      */\\n142:     protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\\n143: \\n144:     private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \\\"numLateRecordsDropped\\\";\\n145: \\n146:     protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\\n147: \\n148:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n149:     // State that is not checkpointed\\n150:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n151: \\n152:     /** The state in which the window contents is stored. \\n292:     @Override\\n293:     public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\\n294:         final Collection<W> elementWindows =\\n295: whose state to discard\\n630:      */\\n631:     protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\\n632:         long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\\n633:         if (cleanupTime ==       triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\\n640:         } else {\\n641:             triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\\n642:         }\\n643:     }\\n644: \\n645:     /**\\n646:      * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\\n647:      *\\n648:      * @param window the window whose state to discard\\n649:      */\\n650:     protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\\n651:         long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\\n652:         if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\\n653:             // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\\n654:             return;\\n655:         }\\n656:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\\n657:             triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\\n658:         } else {\\n659:             triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\\n660:         }\\n661:     }\\n662: \\n663:     /**\\n664:      * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\\n665:      * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\\n666:      * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\\n667:      *\\n668:      * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\\n669:      */\\n670:     private long cleanupTime(W window) {\\n671:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\\n672:             long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\\n673:             return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\\n674:         } else {\\n675:             return window.maxTimestamp();\\n676:         }\\n677:     }\\n678: \\n679:     /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\\n680:     protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\\n681:         return time == cleanupTime(window);\\n682:     }\\n683: \\n684:     /**\\n685:      * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. java.util.Collection;\n77: \n78: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\n79: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n80: \n81: /**\n82:  * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n83:  * Trigger}.\n84:  *\n85:  * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n86:  * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n87:  * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n88:  * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n89:  * WindowAssigner}.\n90:  *\n91:  * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n92:  * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n93:  * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n94:  * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n95:  *\n96:  * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n97:  * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n98:  * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n99:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n100:  */\n101: @Internal\n102: public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n103:         extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n104:         implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n105: \n106:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n107: \n108:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n109:     // Configuration values and user functions\n110:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n111: \n112:     protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n113: \n114:     private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n115: \n116:     private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n117: \n118:     private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n119: \n120:     /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n121:     protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n122: \n123:     /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n124:     protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n125: \n126:     /**\n127:      * The allowed lateness for elements. windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n296:                         element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n297: \n298:         // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n299:         boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n300: \n301:         final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n302: \n303:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n304:             MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n305: \n306:             for (W window : elementWindows) {\n307: \n308:                 // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n309:                 // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n310:                 // actualWindow == window\n311:                 W actualWindow =\n312:                         mergingWindows.addWindow(\n313:                                 window,\n314:                                 new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n315:                                     @Override\n316:                                     public void merge(\n317:                                             W mergeResult,\n318:                                             Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n319:                                             W stateWindowResult,\n320:                                             Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n321:                                             throws Exception {\n322: \n323:                                         if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n324:                                                 && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n325:                                                         <= internalTimerService\n326:                                                                 .currentWatermark())) {\n327:                                             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n328:                                                     \"The end timestamp of an \"\n329:                                                             + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n330:                                                             + \"by merging.                 triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n354: \n355:                                         for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n356:                    }\n360: \n361:                                         // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n362:                                         // state window\n363:            }\n366:                                 });\n367: \n368:                 // drop if the window is already late\n369:                 if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n370:                     mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n371:                     continue;\n372:                 }\n373:                 isSkippedElement = false;\n374: \n375:                 W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n376:                 if (stateWindow == null) {\n377:                     throw new IllegalStateException(\n378:                             \"Window \" + (contents != null) {\n392:                         emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n393:                     }\n394:                 }\n395: \n396:                 if       // drop if the window is already late\n408:                 if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n409:                     continue;\n410:                 }\n411:                 isSkippedElement = false;\n412: \n413:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n414:                 windowState.add(element.getValue());\n415: \n416:                 triggerContext.key = key;\n417:      }\n426:                 }\n427: \n428:                 if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n429:                     windowState.clear();\n430:                 }\n431: this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n445:             }\n446:         }\n447:     }\n448: \n449:     @Override\n450:     public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n451: state to discard\n630:      */\n631:     protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n632:         long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n633:         if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n634: /**\n646:      * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n647:      *\n648:      * @param window the window whose state to discard\n649:      */\n650:     protected void one\n654:             return;\n655:         }\n656:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n657:             triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n658:         } else {\n659:             triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n660:         }\n661:     }\n662: \n663:     /**\n664:      * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp Comparable<Timer<K, W>> {\n941:         protected long timestamp;\n942:         protected K key;\n943:         protected W window;\n944: \n945:         public Timer(long timestamp, K key, W window) {\n946:             this.timestamp = timestamp;\n947:             this.key = key;\n948:             this.window = window;\n949:         }\n950: \n951:         @Override\n952:         public int       result = 31 * result + key.hashCode();\n976:             result = 31 * result + window.hashCode();\n977:             return result;\n978:         }\n979: \n980:         @Override\n981:         + key\n987:                     + \", window=\"\n988:                     + window\n989:                     + '}';\n990:         }\n991:     }\n992: \n993:     // Let me analyze the `processElement` method (lines 292-447):\n\n1. The method first gets the windows assigned to the element (lines 294-296)\n2. For merging windows (lines 303-403):\n   - It iterates over each assigned window\n   - It adds the window to the merging window set, which may result in a merge (lines 311-366)\n   - THEN it checks if the window is late using `isWindowLate()` (line 369)\n   - If it's late, it retires the window and continues to the next window\n   - So far so good for merging windows\n\n3. For non-merging windows (lines 404-433):\n   - It iterates over each window\n   - It checks if the window is late using `isWindowLate()` (line 408)\n   - If it's late, it continues to the next window\n\n4. The side output logic (lines 440-446):\n   - If `isSkippedElement` is true AND `isElementLate()` returns true, it side outputs or drops the element\n   - This is the problem!\n\nThe bug is at the END of the processElement method (lines 440-446):\n```java\n// side output input event if\n// element not handled by any window\n// late arriving tag has been set\n// windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n// element timestamp\nif (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n    if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n        sideOutput(element);\n    } else {\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe problem is that for merging windows:\n1. An element arrives with a timestamp past the watermark but within allowedLateness\n2. The `isElementLate()` method (line 620-624) checks if `element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()`\n\n\n3. The merging window code uses `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` at line 369, which checks if the window itself is expired: `window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= watermark`. An element could technically be late by the element timestamp check, yet still be able to extend an existing session window that hasn't expired yet.\n\nI think the real issue is that the code conflates two different notions of \"lateness\" - whether the element itself is late versus whether the window it would belong to is late.\n\nFor merging windows specifically, the logic at lines 440-446 checks both `isSkippedElement` and `isElementLate(element)`. But an element might be assigned to a window that's already considered late and gets retired at line 370, even though that element could have extended the session. The fundamental problem seems to be that lateness is evaluated before the merging logic has a chance to determine if the element can actually extend an existing window.\n\nLooking at the code flow: an element arrives after the watermark, but for merging windows it should first check whether it can merge into an existing window before classifying it as too late. The current implementation checks `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` after the merge happens, which appears correct, but then at the end it evaluates `isElementLate()` based on the element's timestamp rather than the window's state. The merging windows code iterates through potential windows and handles merges, but the lateness determination doesn't account for whether the element could have extended a session window. . If late, retire it and skip this window\n5. If not late, add the element to the window\n\nThe issue is that a late element might still extend an existing session window, but gets dropped because it's considered late based on the watermark. Looking at the code, when an element is late, it's retired immediately without checking if it could merge with existing windows. The distinction being missed is between an element that truly has no window assignment versus one that could extend a current window through merging. Re-examining the logic more carefully, the actual problem appears more nuanced—in the merging windows path\n\n, windows are assigned and checked for lateness individually. If a window passes the lateness check, `isSkippedElement` is set to false, but if all assigned windows are late, the flag stays true. This means elements that could have extended a session get dropped at lines 440-446 even though they might have been valid for merging purposes.\n\nThe issue appears in how session windows handle late arrivals—when E2 arrives after the watermark but could merge with an existing session from E1, the code evaluates whether the *merged* window exceeds the lateness threshold. If it does, the element gets skipped entirely rather than being added to extend the session, which seems to contradict the intended behavior of session windows that should accommodate late-arriving elements within their gap tolerance.\n\nThe real problem lies in the side output path itself. When an element is classified as late, the code attempts to emit it via `sideOutput()`, which calls `output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element)`. But if `lateDataOutputTag` is null at that point in execution, the side output silently fails without any indication that the late data was dropped.\n\nLooking at the merging window logic more carefully: when checking lateness before the merge operation, an element arriving after the watermark but capable of extending a session window gets incorrectly flagged as late. The element never gets added to any window, and then the side output emission is attempted—but the output tag being null causes the emission to be skipped entirely, losing the data.\n\nThe core issue seems to be the order of operations. For merging windows, the lateness check happens before we determine if the element can actually extend an existing window. In contrast, the non-merging window path checks `isWindowLate()` for each individual window and skips it if late, but the merging path applies a blanket lateness check that prevents the element from being processed at all.\n\nThe real problem: for merging windows, an element that's technically late according to `isElementLate()` might still be valid for extending an existing window, but the current code rejects it outright before that extension can happen.\n\nWhen checking `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` for merged windows, we're testing if the resulting window has already expired by the watermark. This creates issues with session windows specifically—an old session might still be retained due to `allowedLateness > 0`, and a new element could legitimately extend it, yet we discard the element because we consider the window itself \"late.\" The code appears to conflate two different concepts: whether a window has expired versus whether an element should be allowed to modify it.\n\nLooking at the validation logic around line 323, there's a check preventing merges that would create windows earlier than the current watermark—essentially rejecting operations that would produce already-expired results. Yet immediately after at line 369, we retire windows that fail the lateness check. The distinction matters: the first prevents creating invalid states, while the second discards valid data based on window age alone.\n\nThe merge function itself is embedded within the `addWindow()` call, so if it throws an error during the merge operation, that exception propagates upward and halts processing. This means the system prioritizes preventing impossible window states over gracefully handling late arrivals—the merge validation acts as a hard constraint before any element gets assigned.\n\nAfter the merge completes successfully, the subsequent check at line 369 verifies whether the resulting window is late, which should remain false if the merge validation worked as intended. The flow seems sound: validate during merge, then confirm the final window state afterward.\n\nLet me reconsider the test requirements. The scenario involves a session window configured with `sideOutputLateData(tag)` where an element arrives after the watermark passes. For session windows, a new element should merge with an existing window if it falls within the gap time. However, if the watermark has already advanced past the session window's end (element timestamp plus gap duration), the window becomes late and the incoming element gets routed to the side output rather than processed normally.\n\nThe allowedLateness setting determines whether late elements can still be merged: if it's greater than zero, elements within that grace period can still update windows; if zero, they're immediately dropped. I suspect the bug occurs when an element could theoretically merge with an existing window but gets incorrectly sent to the side output because the lateness check at line 440 fires before the merge logic completes. The condition `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))` may be evaluating too early in the process, before determining whether the element can actually merge with an active window.\n\nLooking more closely at the merging window logic around lines 368-372, when a window is deemed late via `isWindowLate(actualWindow)`, it gets retired and the element is skipped. But the non-merging window path at lines 407-410 performs a similar check. The core issue appears to be that the code checks whether the element itself is late rather than whether the window it would merge into is still accepting updates within the allowedLateness window.\n\nThe distinction matters: an element with an old timestamp might be considered late, yet the window it belongs to could still be open for merging if allowedLateness hasn't fully elapsed. The current implementation conflates element lateness with window lateness, potentially dropping elements that should still be processed. This causes elements that could extend existing windows to be dropped as late\n4. The side output emission might not work correctly in this case\n\nLet me trace through a specific scenario:\n- Session window with gap=3, allowedLateness=0\n- Element1 at timestamp 1000 creates a session [1000, 1003]\n- Watermark advances to 4998\n- Element2 at timestamp 4500 arrives\n  - New session would be [4500, 4503]\n  - isWindowLate([4500, 4503]) checks if 4503 + 0 <= 4998, which is TRUE\n  - So the window is considered late and the element is skipped\n  - But the comment in the test says this element should NOT be dropped\n\nSo the issue is clear: the element at 4500 should be processed even though it creates a late window, because it might be merged with other elements later (or with existing windows).\n\nActually, wait. Let me re-read the test more carefully. Maybe I'm misunderstanding when the watermark is at 4998...\n\nLooking at the test code again:\n```java\ntestHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\ntestHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\nexpected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\ntestHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n\n\ntestHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\nexpected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n// this will not be dropped because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n// after the current watermark\ntestHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n```\n\nSo the watermark reaches 4998 before the element at 4500 arrives. The comment indicates this element shouldn't be dropped because the resulting session window would have a max timestamp after the current watermark. With a gap of 3, the element at 4500 creates a window [4500, 4503], and 4503 is indeed greater than 4998, so it shouldn't be considered late.\n\nWait, that doesn't make sense. Let me reconsider what the bug description actually says: \"An element arrives with a timestamp past the current watermark but could extend a session window is incorrectly classified as late.\" This implies the element's timestamp should be greater than the watermark, but here 4500 < 4998. The element is arriving after the watermark has already advanced, which would normally mark it as late.\n\nBut perhaps the real issue is that even though the element is technically late, it could still extend an existing session window, and the code is failing to handle this case properly. The side output emission gets skipped because the output tag hasn't been set up correctly at that point in the execution path. too far from 1003 and 2003 to merge with either of them (gap > 3). The actual bug is that the merging window operator checks lateness before attempting to merge, so an element arriving after the watermark gets incorrectly classified as late and skipped, even though it could extend an existing session window. The side output emission then fails because the output tag is null at that point in the code path.\n\nLooking at the WindowOperator code, I see that late element handling only occurs after both merging and non-merging branches complete (lines 440-446). The side output only triggers if the element wasn't added to any window AND is late. For merging windows specifically, if all candidate windows are late, the element won't be added to any window and should be side-outputted, but the lateness check happens too early in the process.\n\nThere's also a potential issue where merging with an expired window throws an error rather than gracefully handling the late merge scenario. I should focus on understanding what the actual problem statement is asking for rather than getting lost in the implementation details.\n\nThe core issue appears to be that when an element arrives late, it gets marked as late by `isElementLate()`, but then the side output mechanism either fails silently or never gets invoked at all. The element could potentially merge with an existing window that hasn't been cleaned up, but if that merge fails or the window is already expired, the element gets dropped without being properly side-outputted. The configuration with `allowedLateness(Time.seconds(0))` and `sideOutputLateData(lateTag)` should catch these late elements, but something in the code path is preventing them from reaching the side output.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":9,"cache_creation_input_tokens":989,"cache_read_input_tokens":67230,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":989},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"493adb7d-484a-41cf-9b66-fbf1265a6fad","uuid":"1e2910ac-42be-4dcf-81d8-9a383013ad91"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01JbzunKFnKEjTYT8DsZfvkB","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Now I see the issue more clearly. Looking at the logic:\n\n1. `isWindowLate()` - checks if a window has already passed its cleanup time (window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= watermark)\n2. `isElementLate()` - checks if an element's timestamp has passed its deadline (element.timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark)\n\nFor the test case with allowedLateness=0 and watermark=4998, element at 4500:\n- Window [4500, 4503]: isWindowLate([4500, 4503]) = (4503 <= 4998) = true\n- Element: isElementLate(element at 4500) = (4500 <= 4998) = true\n\nSo the window is late (which makes sense - it's already expired). The element is also late (which also makes sense).\n\nBut here's the problem: For merging windows, when an element arrives at 4500 with a late timestamp, we should check if it can be merged with ANY existing window before deciding to drop it. If there's an existing window that hasn't been cleaned up yet, we should allow the merge!\n\nThe current code does call `mergingWindows.addWindow()` which should perform the merge. But then it immediately checks if the result is late and drops it.\n\nThe fix should be:\n1. For merging windows, check if the element could be merged with existing windows FIRST\n2. Only reject the element if it's late AND it can't be merged with any existing window\n3. OR more specifically: if merging results in a window that's already expired, handle that case appropriately for late data\n\nActually, looking at the merge function (lines 323-334), it throws an UnsupportedOperationException if the merge would result in an already-expired window. This is to prevent invalid merges.\n\nSo the issue might be: we're trying to merge a late element with existing windows, and the merge is successful, but then the merged window is late, so we reject it.\n\n\nThe real problem is likely that late elements capable of merging with existing windows should be processed rather than immediately rejected. The merge operation itself should take precedence over the lateness check—if an element can extend or combine with an active window, that merge should happen even if the element's timestamp is technically late. Alternatively, the lateness validation might need to be less strict for merging scenarios, allowing late data to participate in window combinations rather than being sidelined. path where the tag might not be available. Looking at lines 440-446, side output only happens when `isSkippedElement` is true, but for merging windows the element might be added to a late window instead without triggering the skip flag. However, line 369 does skip to the next iteration if the window is late, so the element shouldn't be added. I need to trace through what actually happens when a merging window receives a late element that could have been merged with an existing uncleanup window—the element should be processed into that window rather than rejected, but the current logic might be preventing this. use a different lateness check for merged windows. The simplest approach is to check if a merge actually occurred—if the window returned from adding the element differs from the new window, then a merge happened and we should process it regardless of lateness. This requires determining whether the merge succeeded before applying the standard late-element filtering.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":8,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1340,"cache_read_input_tokens":73604,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1340},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"493adb7d-484a-41cf-9b66-fbf1265a6fad","uuid":"a31ff925-a8a6-4370-a2ff-f749428dfc5c"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01Wax3LLMpNAQuGBiEYDCrJY","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Good. Now I have the actual code. Looking at it, I can identify the exact issue:\n\nFor merging windows (lines 303-403):\n- Line 369 checks `if (isWindowLate(actualWindow))` and rejects the window\n- This happens regardless of whether the element is itself late\n- A late element that arrives could still be merged with an existing window\n\nFor non-merging windows (lines 404-433):\n- Line 408 checks `if (isWindowLate(window))` similarly\n\nThe problem is that for merging windows, a late element should NOT be rejected at line 369 if:\n1. The element itself is late AND\n2. The window is also late (meaning both element and window have passed their deadlines)\n\nIn this case, the element should still be processed because it might merge with other elements that are also late.\n\nThe fix is: For merging windows, if an element is late, we should allow it to be added to the window even if the window is also late. The window will be cleaned up normally later.\n\nActually, let me think about this more carefully. The issue description says:\n\"An element arrives with a timestamp past the current watermark but could extend a session window is incorrectly classified as late and then the side output emission is skipped because the output tag is null at that point in the code path.\"\n\nSo the specific case is:\n1. Element arrives with late timestamp\n2. Element could extend a session window (merge)\n3. But the element is being rejected as too late\n4. So it's not being merged, and it's being side-outputted\n\nLooking at the code flow:\n1. Element at timestamp 4500 arrives when watermark is 4998\n2. Element is assigned to window [4500, 4503]\n3. mergingWindows.addWindow([4500, 4503]) tries to merge\n4. If merging fails, actualWindow = [4500, 4503]\n5. isWindowLate([4500, 4503]) = true (because 4503 <= 4998)\n\n\n6. The window gets rejected and we skip to the next one\n7. isSkippedElement stays true since nothing was processed\n8. Later, isElementLate(element at 4500) = true (because 4500 <= 4998)\n9. Element gets side-outputted\n\nThe test expects this element to be processed, not side-outputted. So the fix is to allow the window at line 369 for merging windows instead of rejecting it. If we don't reject it, the element gets added to the window and isSkippedElement becomes false, preventing the side-output.\n\nBut I need to be careful—if we never check `isWindowLate()`, we could end up with too many windows in memory. The better approach is to check `isWindowLate()` only when the element itself is late. If the element is late, we should still process it and merge it with the window. If the element is not late, then we can reject the window if it's late.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2200,"cache_read_input_tokens":81274,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2200},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"493adb7d-484a-41cf-9b66-fbf1265a6fad","uuid":"725e4574-948a-40b7-bcc7-ef022c4ce102"}

java.util.Collection;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n * Trigger}.\n *\n * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n * WindowAssigner}.\n *\n * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Configuration values and user functions\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n\n    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n\n    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n\n    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n\n    /**\n     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n     *\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    protected final long allowedLateness;\n\n    /**\n     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n     * to this.\n     */\n    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n\n    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n\n    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that is not checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n\n    /**\n     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n     * state is not mergeable.\n     */\n    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n\n    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n\n    /**\n     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n     * timestamp.\n     */\n    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n\n    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n\n    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n\n    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n\n    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n    public WindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(windowFunction);\n\n        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n\n        checkArgument(\n                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n\n        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setup(\n            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n            StreamConfig config,\n            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n\n        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n\n        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n\n        windowAssignerContext =\n                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                    @Override\n                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                    }\n                };\n\n        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n            windowState =\n                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n        }\n\n        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n\n            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n            } else if (windowState != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\n                        \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n            }\n\n            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n            final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n\n            final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n                    new TupleSerializer<>(\n                            typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n\n            final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n                    new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n\n            // get the state that stores the merging sets\n            mergingSetsState =\n                    (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(\n                                    VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n            mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        timestampedCollector = null;\n        triggerContext = null;\n        processContext = null;\n        windowAssignerContext = null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            }\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.collect.Iterables;\n\nimport java.util.Collection;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * A {@link WindowOperator} that also allows an {@link Evictor} to be used.\n *\n * <p>The {@code Evictor} is used to remove elements from a pane before/after the evaluation of\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} and after the window evaluation gets triggered by a {@link\n * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger}.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class EvictingWindowOperator<K, IN, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends WindowOperator<K, IN, Iterable<IN>, OUT, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // these fields are set by the API stream graph builder to configure the operator\n\n    private final Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> evictor;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends ListState<StreamRecord<IN>>, ?>\n            evictingWindowStateDescriptor;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // the fields below are instantiated once the operator runs in the runtime\n\n    private transient EvictorContext evictorContext;\n\n    private transient InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>> evictingWindowState;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    public EvictingWindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends ListState<StreamRecord<IN>>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<Iterable<IN>, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> evictor,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(\n                windowAssigner,\n                windowSerializer,\n                keySelector,\n                keySerializer,\n                null,\n                windowFunction,\n                trigger,\n                allowedLateness,\n                lateDataOutputTag);\n\n        this.evictor = checkNotNull(evictor);\n        this.evictingWindowStateDescriptor = checkNotNull(windowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentProcessingTime()) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                            + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentProcessingTime()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        evictingWindowState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                evictingWindowState.add(element);\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n                evictorContext.key = key;\n                evictorContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n                    if (contents == null) {\n                        // if we have no state, there is nothing to do\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents, evictingWindowState);\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    evictingWindowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // check if the window is already inactive\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                evictingWindowState.add(element);\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n                evictorContext.key = key;\n                evictorContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n                    if (contents == null) {\n                        // if we have no state, there is nothing to do\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    emitWindowContents(window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    evictingWindowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n        evictorContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        evictorContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = null;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n        evictorContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        evictorContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = null;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void emitWindowContents(\n            W window, Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState)\n            throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // Work around type system restrictions...\n        FluentIterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> recordsWithTimestamp =\n                FluentIterable.from(contents)\n                        .transform(\n                                new Function<StreamRecord<IN>, TimestampedValue<IN>>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public TimestampedValue<IN> apply(StreamRecord<IN> input) {\n                                        return TimestampedValue.from(input);\n                                    }\n                                });\n        evictorContext.evictBefore(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        FluentIterable<IN> projectedContents =\n                recordsWithTimestamp.transform(\n                        new Function<TimestampedValue<IN>, IN>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public IN apply(TimestampedValue<IN> input) {\n                                return input.getValue();\n                            }\n                        });\n\n        processContext.window = triggerContext.window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key,\n                triggerContext.window,\n                processContext,\n                projectedContents,\n                timestampedCollector);\n        evictorContext.evictAfter(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        // work around to fix FLINK-4369, remove the evicted elements from the windowState.\n        // this is inefficient, but there is no other way to remove elements from ListState, which\n        // is an AppendingState.\n        windowState.update(\n                recordsWithTimestamp.stream()\n                        .map(TimestampedValue::getStreamRecord)\n                        .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n    }\n\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code EvictorContext} is a utility for handling {@code Evictor} invocations. It can be\n     * reused by setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept\n     * in the {@code EvictorContext}.\n     */\n    class EvictorContext implements Evictor.EvictorContext {\n\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        public EvictorContext(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n            return EvictingWindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n        }\n\n        public K getKey() {\n            return key;\n        }\n\n        void evictBefore(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictBefore((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n\n        void evictAfter(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictAfter((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        evictorContext = new EvictorContext(null, null);\n        evictingWindowState =\n                (InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>>)\n                        getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, evictingWindowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        evictorContext = null;\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Getters for testing\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> getEvictor() {\n        return evictor;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked, rawtypes\")\n    public StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, Iterable<IN>>, ?> getStateDescriptor() {\n        return (StateDescriptor<?  OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-6\", 10L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-20\", 5501L, 9050L), 9049));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works correctly with the CountTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithCountTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int
